_meta.model: facebook/convnext-tiny-224 (ALL findings from this model only)
_meta.epistemics_warning: ⚠️ All findings from rigorous 3-run ablation. However, still 1 model, 1 device. CPU behavior can differ significantly between x86 and ARM (Oryon). Check architecture before applying rules.

findings.title: opset 19+ causes 3-10x slowdown on models with Transpose-heavy graphs (ConvNext + DINOv2 confirmed) — NOT ConvNext-specific
findings.observation: ConvNext: opset17=43.7ms, opset19=160ms (3.7x), opset21=170ms (3.9x). DINOv2-small catalog_cpu_sweep 2026-06-18: baseline (auto-config)=112.6ms, opset19=1106ms (9.8x CPU001_REGRESSION), opset21=1095ms (9.7x). CRITICAL: cpu-001 is NOT ConvNext-specific. DINOv2 is a pure-ViT model with no ConvNext architecture overlap. ResNet-18: opset17=237ms, opset19=231ms (+2.4% neutral), opset21=226ms (+4.5% neutral) — ResNet NOT affected. MobileViT: opset19=-9.1%, opset21=-7.4% (mild slowdown, not catastrophic). Pattern: models with dense Transpose usage (DINOv2, ConvNext) hit cpu-001; models with sparse Transpose (ResNet) do not.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: Original hypothesis: ORT C++ Transpose Optimizer has a kMaxSupportedOpset gate (optimizer_api.h). If model opset > kMaxSupportedOpset, Transpose Optimizer is skipped silently. ConvNext has 42 Transpose nodes — without optimization, each executes as a full memory-layout copy. HOWEVER: the non-monotonic recovery at opset 22 (85ms vs 160-170ms at opset 19-21) is inconsistent with a simple binary gate. If the gate fires for opset > N, opset 22 should behave identically to opset 19. The actual mechanism is more complex. Additionally, ORT 1.24.x has kMaxSupportedOpset >= 23 confirmed (separate NHWC gate) — the Transpose Optimizer gate threshold may differ but is unverified.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: For CPU EP: default to opset 17. The empirical data (1 model, consistent across opsets) is unambiguous — opset 17 is the best option. Do NOT try opset 19+. The mechanism reason is uncertain but the practical conclusion is solid.
findings.confidence: high on empirical observation (consistent data across opsets for 1 model). Low on mechanism — the gate hypothesis does not fully explain the non-monotonic opset 22 partial recovery.
findings.scope: Models with dense Transpose graphs (ConvNext + DINOv2 confirmed). ResNet-18 is NOT affected. MobileViT mildly affected. BERT/RoBERTa unknown (sweep in progress 2026-06-18).

ort_kMaxSupportedOpset_by_version.note: These values are for the NHWC layout_transformation gate, NOT the Transpose Optimizer gate. The two constants may differ within the same ORT release.
ort_kMaxSupportedOpset_by_version.v1.24.x: >= 23 (confirmed for NHWC gate; Transpose Optimizer gate unknown)

findings.do_not_generalize_to: QNN NPU EP or DML EP — kMaxSupportedOpset is a CPU-only ORT optimizer gate. These EPs have their own kernel dispatch unaffected by this.
findings.pending: BERT/RoBERTa/MiniLM (sweep in progress 2026-06-18 — expected: neutral based on few Transpose nodes)

findings.title: matmul_add_fusion is a CONFIRMED REGRESSION on ConvNext CPU (+38ms, ~87%)
findings.observation: matmul_add_fusion: p50=81.7ms, runs=[63.0, 70.8, 111.2ms]. Baseline p50=43.7ms. All 3 runs far above highest baseline run (45.4ms).
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: ORT baseline already converts MatMul+Add→Gemm (37 Gemm in model.onnx). Applying matmul_add_fusion on top may create redundant kernel dispatch or conflicting operator mapping. Requires profiling to confirm.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do NOT apply matmul_add_fusion for CPU EP on models where baseline already uses Gemm (check model.onnx for Gemm nodes before applying this pass).
findings.confidence: high — 3 independent runs, all far above baseline; direction is unambiguous
findings.scope: ConvNext and models where ORT L2 baseline already fuses MatMul+Add→Gemm
findings.do_not_generalize_to: Models where baseline does NOT have Gemm (the pass may legitimately help there)

findings.title: transpose_optimizer is neutral on ConvNext CPU (NOT +270ms as previously reported)
findings.observation: winml perf (warmup=10, iter=50): 42.3 / 52.3 / 41.8ms — overlapping baseline. Earlier winml eval-based measurement showed +270ms — this was a measurement artifact.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: winml eval includes HF preprocessing + model load + no warmup. The +270ms was preprocessing overhead, not inference regression. Pure inference measurement (winml perf) shows no effect.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: transpose_optimizer is neutral for ConvNext CPU — neither helpful nor harmful. Can be omitted from search space.
findings.confidence: high — measurement methodology confirmed; tool comparison validated
findings.falsified_by: Earlier winml eval measurement — RETRACTED. Use winml perf for all latency comparisons.
findings.measurement_lesson: Always use winml perf (warmup=10, iter=50) for latency experiments. Never use winml eval latency to compare configs.

findings.title: nchwc_transformer is neutral on ConvNext CPU
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: NCHWc SIMD layout benefits Conv-heavy models. ConvNext has 22 Conv nodes but 57.7% of kernel time is Gemm. The bottleneck is not memory layout but compute throughput — NCHWc doesn't help.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: nchwc_transformer is low-priority for ConvNext-class models. Profile first — if Conv% > 40%, try nchwc. If Gemm% > 50%, skip.
findings.confidence: medium — 3 runs, neutral result; mechanism is a hypothesis

findings.title: Baseline (no extra flags) is the optimal config for ConvNext CPU
findings.observation: No flag in 22-experiment ablation improved p50 beyond noise. Baseline p50=43.7ms is the floor.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: ORT L2 baseline already applies gelu_fusion and MatMul→Gemm before any user flags. The effective optimization space is narrow for ConvNext on CPU. Compute bottleneck (Gemm=57.7%) is not addressable via graph passes.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: For CPU EP on ConvNext-class models: skip optimization pass sweep. Go directly to quantization experiments.
findings.do_not_generalize_to: BERT/Transformer models where attention_fusion + skip_layer_norm can significantly help

findings.title: CPU EP opset 21 is 3.9x SLOWER — opposite of QNN NPU behavior
findings.observation: CPU opset 21: p50=170ms. CPU opset 17: p50=43.7ms. QNN NPU opset 21 (DINOv2): p50=26ms (~24% FASTER than opset 17 at 34ms). Note: the NPU and CPU experiments used DIFFERENT models (CPU=ConvNext, NPU=DINOv2) — the comparison is directional only, not quantitative.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: CPU regression from Transpose Optimizer bypass (see cpu-001 — mechanism uncertain). QNN NPU speedup from unknown cause (original Transpose bypass hypothesis invalidated; Transpose counts identical in opset17/21 graphs). The key insight is that CPU and QNN NPU respond oppositely to opset changes, regardless of the root cause.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: EP ISOLATION: CPU opset findings MUST NOT influence QNN NPU search space, and vice versa. Always validate per EP independently.
findings.confidence: high on empirical observation. Low on mechanism for both directions.
findings.scope: ALL — this is a meta-rule about EP isolation, not model-specific

findings.title: matmul_transpose_fusion gives +92% speedup on ResNet-18 CPU EP (237ms -> 17.8ms)
findings.confidence: high — KEEP_CONFIRMED (all 5 sessions passed Phase C)
findings.scope: Conv-dominant models with MatMul+Transpose sequences. ResNet-18 confirmed. DINOv2 tested but ALL fusion flags regressed (cpu-001 interference). MobileViT partial.
findings.observation: catalog_cpu_sweep 2026-06-18: ResNet-18 h9 (opset17+matmul_transpose_fusion): median_p50=17.797ms vs baseline 237.472ms = +92.51% KEEP_CONFIRMED. Also: h12 (transpose_optimizer) +84.46% KEEP_CONFIRMED, h13 (gelu_fusion) +88.89% KEEP_CONFIRMED, h10 (bundle) +91.54% KEEP_CONFIRMED, h6 (layer_norm_fusion) +10.43% KEEP_CONFIRMED. All 5 phase-C sessions passed.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: ResNet-18 on CPU at default config has 237ms latency (extremely slow for a tiny model). matmul_transpose_fusion folds MatMul+Transpose into a transposed GEMM call, enabling BLAS-level fused execution. ORT CPU provider has a highly optimized transposed-matmul path. The baseline 237ms suggests the default config exports with a suboptimal graph (possibly unfused MatMul+Transpose pairs that prevent BLAS dispatch).
findings.baseline_note: ResNet-18 baseline=237ms on CPU is extremely slow (17.8ms after optimization = 13x speedup). This suggests the default auto-config for ResNet-18 on CPU is severely suboptimal. The baseline uses auto-config which may not be correctly detecting the model architecture for CPU optimization.
findings.autoconfig_action: For ResNet-18 class models on CPU: apply matmul_transpose_fusion (h9) + transpose_optimizer (h12) + gelu_fusion (h13) bundle. Test h10 bundle for single combined build.

findings.title: layer_norm_fusion causes catastrophic -997% regression on MobileViT CPU EP (73ms -> 803ms)
findings.scope: CNN-ViT hybrid models where layer_norm_fusion mismatches the LN implementation. MobileViT confirmed. Pure transformer (BERT/ViT) expected safe.
findings.observation: catalog_cpu_sweep 2026-06-18: MobileViT h6 (opset17+layer_norm_fusion): median_p50=803.217ms vs baseline 73.166ms = -997.8% DISCARD. 3-session consistent. For comparison: bias_softmax_fusion (h7) = 64.137ms (+12.34% MARGINAL_UNCONFIRMED). layer_norm_fusion, skip_layer_norm_fusion, attention_fusion, matmul_transpose_fusion all severely regress MobileViT on CPU.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: MobileViT uses a hybrid CNN-ViT architecture where LayerNorm is placed after Conv2D outputs. layer_norm_fusion expects pure transformer LN sequences (MLP-style). Fusing the wrong LN pattern creates a combined op that the CPU runtime cannot dispatch to an optimized kernel path, forcing fallback to element-wise operations.
findings.autoconfig_action: Block layer_norm_fusion for CNN-ViT hybrid models. Also block matmul_transpose_fusion and attention_fusion for MobileViT-class models on CPU. analyze_insight.py should detect CNN-ViT hybrid architecture and skip these fusions.

findings.title: cpu-001 opset regression fires on DINOv2 pure-ViT: ~10x slowdown at opset19/21 on CPU EP
findings.confidence: high — CPU001_REGRESSION verdict confirmed (pattern matches ConvNext)
findings.scope: Pure-ViT models with dense Transpose graphs on CPU EP. DINOv2-small confirmed. BERT/NLP expected neutral (sparse Transpose). ResNet-18 confirmed neutral.
findings.observation: catalog_cpu_sweep 2026-06-18: DINOv2-small h2 (opset19): 1106ms vs baseline 112ms (-882% CPU001_REGRESSION). h3 (opset21): 1095ms (-873%). h4 attention_fusion: 1083ms (-862%). h7 bias_softmax_fusion: 1121ms (-896%). The baseline (auto-config, opset not forced) = 112ms. Any forced opset or attention-style fusion causes catastrophic regression. Also: h1 opset17-explicit = 762ms (-577%) — even forcing opset17 explicitly regresses DINOv2 vs auto-config baseline.
findings.mechanism_note: DINOv2 has 169 Reshape nodes in opset21 vs 121 in opset17. Dense Transpose (49 nodes). cpu-001 mechanism (Transpose Optimizer bypass) applies here as strongly as ConvNext. The auto-config baseline (h0) at 112ms is already the optimized path; ANY deviation from auto-config triggers regression.
findings.autoconfig_action: For DINOv2/ViT-class on CPU EP: use auto-config default opset ONLY. Do not force any opset. Do not apply attention_fusion or bias_softmax_fusion (all regress DINOv2 on CPU). CPU EP for DINOv2 is constrained to baseline config only.

skip: 19, 20, 21, 22 — kMaxSupportedOpset regression (cpu-001). Only safe to try if ORT version's kMaxSupportedOpset >= target.
opset.dialectical_note: ⚠️ This rule is ORT-version dependent. Check kMaxSupportedOpset for the shipping ORT build before skipping higher opsets.

quantization.recommended: w8a8 (CPU benefits most from small model size)
quantization.dialectical_note: ⚠️ W8A8 on CPU not yet validated for ConvNext. General guidance — run accuracy gate.

compile.dialectical_note: ⚠️ winml compile targets QNN EPContext. Not applicable to CPU EP.

skip: matmul_add_fusion if model already has Gemm (cpu-002)
graph_passes.dialectical_note: ⚠️ Skip rules are Gemm-bottleneck specific. Conv-heavy models may still benefit from nchwc_transformer.

meta_lessons.measurement_discipline: Always use winml perf (warmup=10, iter=50) for latency. Never use winml eval latency. See cpu-003.
meta_lessons.ep_isolation: CPU findings (especially opset regression) DO NOT transfer to QNN NPU or DML. Each EP has its own optimizer path. See cpu-006.
meta_lessons.baseline_check: Before applying any fusion flag, check model.onnx for existing fused ops. If Gemm already present, matmul_add_fusion is likely a no-op or regression.
